Notice Of Hearing On And Petition To Compel Contractual Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Completion
Off Calendar Feb-07-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS MICHAEL RUPPRECHT LAW, MICHAEL RUPPRECHT, GVM LAW, LLP's Petition To Compel Contractual Arbitration And Stay Action Pending Completion

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
William Keith, Esq. of KLEIN & WILSON, Tel:949.631.3300, for plaintiff;
No other appearances.

Plaintiff's counsel represented to the court that opposing counsel does not contest the tentative ruling. There being no objection, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants' motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. A party seeking to compel arbitration always bears the burden to establish the existence of a written agreement to arbitrate by a preponderance of the evidence. (See Rosenthal v. Great W. Fin. Sec. Corp. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 394, 413.) Here, defendants fail to maintain that burden. The only identified arbitration agreement pertains to an unrelated "construction agreement." (Notice of Motion, Ex. 1; Callahan Decl., pars. 12-13.) Defendants fail to show that the parties entered into an enforceable arbitration agreement that applies to this action. In fact, defendants' evidence to prove such agreement/s is based on information and belief. (Rupprecth Decl., par. 9; Bowden v. Robinson (1977) 67 Cal.App.3d 705, 719-720 [declarations based on information and belief show a lack of personal knowledge and are inadmissible]; Sweetwater Union High School Dist. v. Gilbane Bldg. Co. (2019) 6 Cal.5th 931, 945 [declaration must be based on personal knowledge].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sarah MacDevitt, CSR# 14175, Email: sarahseitzcsr@gmail.com, Tel: 415.955.7843. (302/RBU)
